untranslatable_reason: Signal requires real-time assignment probability calculation on short call and dynamic exercise decision logic based on probabilistic thresholds. Assignment probability is a derivatives-specific metric not available in standard OHLCV indicators. Additionally, the rule requires conditional logic (IF probability < threshold THEN exercise put ELSE let expire) that cannot be expressed in the allowed 3-field condition format. The strike price (100) and position structure (conversion: short call + long put + long underlying) are contract-level metadata, not price-based indicators.
translation_notes: This is an event-driven position management rule at expiration requiring real-time assignment probability data and conditional exercise decisions. It belongs in a derivatives trading engine with access to options Greeks and assignment probability models, not a price-action backtest engine. The signal can only be approximated by checking if price is at strike at expiration (close == 100), but this misses the core logic: probability-based assignment risk assessment and dynamic hedging decisions.
